action.reasoning: Observation: Iteration 11 generated an overfitted ensemble_weights.json (weights [0.0, 0.8, 0.2]) by tuning on a training subset; it discards the strongest single model (raw ConvNeXt, 72.67% test) and will likely degrade test performance. We have ~35 minutes and 19 iterations left. Analysis: The best verified score is 74.60% from a uniform 3-model ensemble with TenCrop TTA. A cheap way to potentially improve further is to expand the ensemble with the 'final' checkpoints from each training run (raw, cleaned, ResNet). These checkpoints offer slightly different weight states and can increase prediction diversity without any training cost. Including 6 models total (best+final from each run) with uniform weights is safe because none of the checkpoints are near-random. Decision: 1) Delete the bad ensemble_weights.json. 2) Update predict.py to load all 6 available checkpoints and average their logits uniformly with TenCrop TTA. 3) Run a quick local sanity check on val.pt. 4) Submit evaluation.
